title: Why didn't the Buddha return to lay life.To play the media you will need to either update your browser to a recent version or update yourFlash plugin
justification: This talk directly addresses the chapter theme in its title and opening question, providing the most focused and comprehensive exploration of why the Buddha did not return to lay life. It offers practical reasons (the wandering life, formation of the sangha), spiritual reasons (nothing attracts at that level of realization), and crucially bridges to lay practice by explaining how the Buddha's teachings can transform ordinary daily activities into spiritual practice. The talk has excellent flow, moving from the Buddha's choice through to practical applications of impermanence, dukkha, and anatta in everyday life.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma explores why the Buddha remained in the homeless life after enlightenment, examining both practical circumstances and the spiritual reality that nothing in worldly life attracts one who has reached full realization. The talk then demonstrates how the Buddha's teachings enable lay practitioners to transform ordinary activities into spiritual practice through right understanding of impermanence, suffering, and not-self.

relevance.note: Directly addresses why Buddha remained in monastic life
